storyboard.content: I have severe pain in my right side and back that came on suddenly about 6 hours ago. It's the worst pain I've ever felt and it comes in waves.
I've noticed my urine is pink or red — it's clearly got blood in it. That really scared me.
I've been vomiting multiple times and I feel extremely nauseous. I can't keep anything down.
I feel an intense urge to urinate very frequently but when I try, almost nothing comes out. It's really frustrating on top of everything else.
Everything started suddenly about 6 hours ago with no warning. There was nothing gradual about it — it just hit me all at once.
The pain is a 9 out of 10 — I literally cannot find a comfortable position. I've been pacing, sitting, lying down, nothing helps. I'm completely incapacitated.
I'm sweating a lot even though I'm not hot, and the nausea is constant. The pain waves are so intense that I feel like I might pass out.
I'm a 43-year-old male with no major health conditions that I know of. This came out of nowhere with no prior episodes like this.
